You
You are a developer migrating from Git who just pulled the latest changes from a remote repository. The local repository is located at `/home/user/repo` and is cloned from a remote at `/home/user/remote_repo`. After running `jj git fetch`, you notice that the bookmark `feature-x` has diverged because both you and a teammate pushed conflicting commits to the same bookmark. You need to verify the local and remote bookmarks after the sync, and resolve the divergence by forcing the local `feature-x` bookmark to point to the remote's version (`feature-x@origin`), discarding your local changes to that bookmark. Your tasks are: 1. Change directory to `/home/user/repo`. 2. Fetch the latest changes from the remote `origin`. 3. Resolve the diverged bookmark `feature-x` so that it points exactly to the commit of `feature-x@origin`. 4. Ensure that the bookmark `feature-x` no longer has a conflict/divergence.

🎉 Task Completed
I have successfully resolved the diverged bookmark `feature-x` in the Jujutsu repository.

Here's a summary of the steps taken:
1. **Navigated to the repository:** Changed directory to `/home/user/repo`.
2. **Fetched latest changes:** Ran `jj git fetch` to sync with the remote repository. This updated `feature-x@origin` and confirmed the divergence of the local `feature-x` bookmark.
3. **Identified the divergence:** Used `jj bookmark list` to verify that `feature-x` was in a conflicted state, with both local and remote changes.
4. **Resolved the divergence:** Ran `jj bookmark set feature-x --revision feature-x@origin --allow-backwards` to force the local `feature-x` bookmark to point exactly to the remote version (`feature-x@origin`), effectively discarding the local changes to that bookmark.
5. **Updated working copy:** Ran `jj edit feature-x` to move the working copy to the now-updated `feature-x` bookmark.
6. **Verified the resolution:** Confirmed with `jj bookmark list` that the bookmark `feature-x` is no longer conflicted and matches the remote version.

The repository is now in a clean state with `feature-x` pointing to the remote's version.

I'm going to test it, and then check the help files associated with it to better understand its parameters and options before moving forward.\n\n\n","state":"done"},{"type":"tool-executeCommand","toolCallId":"EBSkRAfO9XPjoVUi","state":"output-available","input":{"command":"cd /home/user/repo && jj help bookmark set"},"output":{"output":"Create or update a bookmark to point to a certain commit\r\n\r\nUsage: jj bookmark set [OPTIONS] <NAMES>...\r\n\r\nArguments:\r\n  <NAMES>...\r\n          The bookmarks to update\r\n\r\nOptions:\r\n  -r, --revision <REVSET>\r\n          The bookmark's target revision\r\n          \r\n          [aliases: to]\r\n\r\n  -B, --allow-backwards\r\n          Allow moving the bookmark backwards or sideways\r\n\r\n  -h, --help\r\n          Print help (see a summary with '-h')\r\n\r\nGlobal Options:\r\n  -R, --repository <REPOSITORY>\r\n          Path to repository to operate on\r\n          \r\n          By default, Jujutsu searches for the closest .jj/ directory in an ancestor of the current\r\n          working directory.\r\n\r\n      --ignore-working-copy\r\n          Don't snapshot the working copy, and don't update it\r\n          \r\n          By default, Jujutsu snapshots the working copy at the beginning of every command. The\r\n          working copy is also updated at the end of the command, if the command modified the\r\n          working-copy commit (`@`). If you want to avoid snapshotting the working copy and instead\r\n          see a possibly stale working-copy commit, you can use `--ignore-working-copy`. This may be\r\n          useful e.g. in a command prompt, especially if you have another process that commits the\r\n          working copy.\r\n          \r\n          Loading the repository at a specific operation with `--at-operation` implies\r\n          `--ignore-working-copy`.\r\n\r\n      --ignore-immutable\r\n          Allow rewriting immutable commits\r\n          \r\n          By default, Jujutsu prevents rewriting commits in the configured set of immutable commits.\r\n          This option disables that check and lets you rewrite any commit but the root commit.\r\n          \r\n          This option only affects the check. It does not affect the `immutable_heads()` revset or\r\n          the `immutable` template keyword.\r\n\r\n      --at-operation <AT_OPERATION>\r\n          Operation to load the repo at\r\n          \r\n          Operation to load the repo at. By default, Jujutsu loads the repo at the most recent\r\n          operation, or at the merge of the divergent operations if any.\r\n          \r\n          You can use `--at-op=<operation ID>` to see what the repo looked like at an earlier\r\n          operation. For example `jj --at-op=<operation ID> st` will show you what `jj st` would\r\n          have shown you when the given operation had just finished. `--at-op=@` is pretty much the\r\n          same as the default except that divergent operations will never be merged.\r\n          \r\n          Use `jj op log` to find the operation ID you want. Any unambiguous prefix of the operation\r\n          ID is enough.\r\n          \r\n          When loading the repo at an earlier operation, the working copy will be ignored, as if\r\n          `--ignore-working-copy` had been specified.\r\n          \r\n          It is possible to run mutating commands when loading the repo at an earlier operation.\r\n          Doing that is equivalent to having run concurrent commands starting at the earlier\r\n          operation. There's rarely a reason to do that, but it is possible.\r\n          \r\n          [aliases: at-op]\r\n\r\n      --debug\r\n          Enable debug logging\r\n\r\n      --color <WHEN>\r\n          When to colorize output\r\n          \r\n          [possible values: always, never, debug, auto]\r\n\r\n      --quiet\r\n          Silence non-primary command output\r\n          \r\n          For example, `jj file list` will still list files, but it won't tell you if the working\r\n          copy was snapshotted or if descendants were rebased.\r\n          \r\n          Warnings and errors will still be printed.\r\n\r\n      --no-pager\r\n          Disable the pager\r\n\r\n      --config <NAME=VALUE>\r\n          Additional configuration options (can be repeated)\r\n          \r\n          The name should be specified as TOML dotted keys. The value should be specified as a TOML\r\n          expression.
